title: Aulonemia setosa
taxonomy: Aulonemia setosa is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Bambuseae and the genus Aulonemia. The classification of Aulonemia setosa is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Monocots, clade: Commelinids, order: Poales, family: Poaceae, subfamily: Bambusoideae, tribe: Bambuseae, and genus: Aulonemia. Aulonemia setosa is native to South America. Further classification of the species is Aulonemia setosa, subgenus: Aulonemia, subtribe: Bambusinae.

intro: Aulonemia setosa, commonly known as Setosagrass, is a type of grass native to the Andean region of South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Aulonemia setosa include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Aulonemia setosa is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support immune function.
